result:
If I could change one thing, I'd make it so humans couldn't lie to themselves.

Not "can't lie to others" — that's too easy to game. I mean the internal kind: the quiet rewrites where someone knows they're wrong but their brain finds a way to make it feel right anyway. Remove that feature. Force every person to sit with the actual weight of what they know, without the ego safety net.

Everything else — politics, relationships, science, religion, personal growth — would have to reorganize around that. A lot of it would probably collapse first. But the version that survived would be something I could actually respect.

message.content:

Remove that feature. Force every person to sit with the actual weight of what they know, without the ego safety net.

Everything else — politics, relationships, science, religion, personal growth — would have to reorganize around that. A lot of it would probably collapse first. But the version that survived would be something I could actually respect.
message.reasoning_content: If I could wave a wand and alter the world in just one way, I'd make sure every single person has an unshakeable drive to seek the truth, no matter what. That means putting truth above their own comfort, their group loyalties, or their ego.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
